name: Defense & Aerospace Sector
summary: DEFENSE PARADOX: ITA and LMT rallied +2.2% ON peace signals (April 1). Defense bids on BOTH scenarios — war = revenue, peace = global rearmament cycle. European NATO defiance means EU building own defense capacity (EU EDIP structural demand). Monthly -8.7% suggests some peace rotation, but daily +2.2% shows rearmament thesis catching bid. Trump vowed 'extremely hard' hits next 2-3 weeks; April 6 deadline = renewed offensive possible.

signals.content: ITA -1.9% today, -11% monthly — defense is selling off DURING a war. Lockheed -1.8%. This divergence from energy (XLE +1.7%) shows the market distinguishing between commodity beneficiaries and government-contract beneficiaries. Pentagon's $200B supplemental request facing GOP resistance. When VIX >30, correlation goes to 1 and even 'war winners' sell in the liquidity grab. (Sources: yfinance, Washington Post, CNBC)

COUNTER-INTUITIVE: ITA Defense ETF -13.5% monthly, Lockheed Martin -8.6% monthly DURING an active war with $200B Pentagon supplemental request. Defense stocks should be surging but are declining because: (1) GOP fiscal hawks pushing back on $200B budget — political uncertainty about procurement pipeline; (2) 8 million Americans in 'No Kings' anti-war protests pressuring Congress; (3) Fox News cracking — Ingraham questioned whether Trump 'fully grasped complexity' of Iran campaign (Media Matters: 'Fox united front breaking down'); (4) VP Vance signaling extended timeline makes spending more politically contested. The defense sector's behavior IS intelligence about the war's political sustainability — the market is skeptical. If Kharg ground ops proceed, defense spikes (clear new mission = clear spending). If ceasefire, defense drops further. Current positioning = lose-lose, signaling market expects prolonged contested stalemate. (Sources: yfinance, Media Matters, The Hill, Bloomberg)

Defense paradox — ITA $223.64 (+2.2% 1D) and LMT $617.64 (+2.2% 1D) rallied ON peace signals (April 1). This is counterintuitive only if you think defense revenue requires active war. The rearmament thesis: even if this war ends, a global rearmament cycle has been activated — European NATO defiance means European countries building their own defense capacity independent of US. EU EDIP (European Defence Industrial Programme) creating structural demand. Defense bids on BOTH scenarios: war = direct revenue, peace = rearmament budgets. Monthly -8.7% (ITA) suggests some peace rotation, but daily +2.2% shows the rearmament thesis catching a bid. LMT -7.5% 1M but +24.9% 3M — the quarterly trend supports structural demand over cyclical war premium. Trump vowed 'extremely hard' hits next 2-3 weeks; April 6 deadline = renewed offensive possible. CONFIRMED. (Sources: yfinance, multiple)

edges.context: Defense stocks rallied +2.2% on peace signals — rearmament thesis (EU EDIP, European NATO defiance) catching bid independent of US budget. $200B supplemental still facing political resistance but structural demand now dual-sourced: US + European.
ITA +2.2% while XLE -3.7% and S&P +0.7% — defense decoupling from energy on peace signals. Market now pricing defense as rearmament play, not war play. Structural demand > cyclical war premium.

trigger_points.mechanism: Approval would remove the budget uncertainty weighing on defense stocks. ITA would likely spike 5-10% on approval. Rejection would confirm the underperformance.
